topic: Optimize blind-box categories and item lists for Game content extraction

top_ideas.title: 20 common scene entries
top_ideas.description: Replace broad legacy themes with 20 more intuitive scene entry points.

top_ideas.title: short scene-plus-purpose names
top_ideas.description: Use compact names that fit a small Chinese UI and remain easy to scan.

top_ideas.title: five-layer item pool model
top_ideas.description: Split each category into core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, conditional_items, and blocked_or_risky.

top_ideas.title: keep current runtime buckets for compatibility
top_ideas.description: Retain large, medium, small, and hanging in the short term, but redefine their source semantics.

top_ideas.title: underwater-diving pilot
top_ideas.description: Preserve the underwater theme while excluding glow, reflection, transparency, and animal bodies.

primary_recommendation: Use the 20 category scene model and the five-layer item pools as the target content model, starting with three pilots before any full-library rewrite.
implementation_path: Rewrite three pilots: desk-study, underwater-diving, and park-picnic.
Map the five-layer model back to the current runtime buckets.
Run sample extraction reviews and record mismatch causes.
Expand the remaining categories only after pilots meet quality targets.
